subject: Project Management
title: The Certification Debate
description: A 60-second lesson on The Certification Debate.

hook.text: You can become a Certified ScrumMaster in two days and $1,500. Critics call it a rubber stamp.

buildup.text: The Scrum Alliance and SAFe certifications have generated billions in training revenue.

discovery.text: Supporters say certification raises the bar. Critics say it commodifies a mindset into a checklist.

twist.text: Some of the original manifesto signatories openly oppose the certification industry.

climax.text: Agile was born rejecting rigid process — now it has its own rigid credentialing system.

punchline.text: The anti-process movement built a process industry.

quiz.question: Why do critics oppose Agile certifications?
options: They commodify a mindset into a checklist
